trip.title: Rome April 2026
trip.trip_purpose: solo cultural immersion — food, wandering, one special dinner

cards.title: Caffè Teichner
cards.address: Piazza di S. Lorenzo in Lucina, 15, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: Walk ~12–15 min north toward Piazza San Lorenzo in Lucina → Stand at bar → Cappuccino + cornetto → Continue north toward Villa Borghese.
details.notes:
## ☕ Morning Coffee [7:00 – 8:30] | Teichner → Villa Borghese

**Primary:** Caffè Teichner
**Alternative (closer / slower start):** Two Sizes

**Flow**

- Walk ~12–15 min north toward Piazza San Lorenzo in Lucina
- Stand at bar (keep it quick and Roman)
- Order: cappuccino + cornetto
- Continue north toward Villa Borghese

**Why this spot**

- Natural directional stop on the way to Borghese
- Elegant piazza setting
- Creates a clean "launch point" for the park morning

**Duration:** 30–45 mins
**Cost:** €3–6 per person
**Energy:** Light, purposeful

cards.title: Villa Borghese Morning
cards.address: 00197 Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy

details.flow: 9:00 AM – Enter park, casual walk, tree-lined paths, no strict route. 9:45 AM – Pincian Terrace Overlook: view over Piazza del Popolo, great photo without crowds, 10–15 min. Optional: coffee stop, short bike rental loop, or just sit on a bench.
details.prep: Comfortable walking shoes. No tickets required unless visiting the gallery. Light layer for morning shade.
details.tips: Arrive early to avoid tour groups. Don't over-structure. Leave feeling refreshed not tired.
details.notes:
**Intent**
A slow, restorative morning in Rome's most elegant park — the antidote to packed monuments, designed to be felt rather than ticked off.

**Know before you go**
- Enter from Porta Pinciana (top of Via Veneto) for the most dramatic arrival through the tree canopy
- The Pincian Terrace overlook is a 10-minute walk from the main entrance — worth going there first before the light shifts
- No tickets, no queues, no agenda — resist the urge to fill it
- Bike rentals are available inside the park if you want to cover more ground without effort
- The park connects directly downhill to Piazza del Popolo if you want to extend the morning

cards.title: Pastry & Tiramisu Class
cards.address: Via Soriso 68/A, 00166, Rome

details.notes:
**Intent**
A hands-on pastry and tiramisu class — loose, social, and Prosecco-at-the-start relaxed. The warm-up before Monti and Ai Tre Scalini, not the main event.

**Know before you go**
- Arrive by 5:20 PM sharp — they hold the door 10 minutes maximum
- Bring the QR tickets (codes Rj2Cuba and WT8nDny); screenshots work fine
- Do not eat a full lunch; you'll taste as you go and dinner is at 8:30
- Wear something you don't mind getting flour on; aprons are provided but not generous
- The pistachio tiramisu is the class signature — pay attention to the mascarpone-whipping step, it is the one technique that actually transfers home
- Walk distance to Ai Tre Scalini in Monti is 25 minutes — comfortable, or cab if you are tired
details.cancellation_policy: Full refund if cancelled via email at least 48 hours before the experience; transaction fee of €1.00 retained. No refund for cancellations within 48 hours of start.

cards.title: Ai Tre Scalini
cards.address: Via Panisperna, 251, 00184 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A beloved Monti wine bar that earns its reputation quietly — the right place to sink into the neighbourhood on your first real Roman evening.

**Know before you go**
- No reservations — arrive by 7:30 PM or expect to wait
- Order the cheese and salumi board alongside whatever natural wine they recommend; the list changes regularly
- Monti is Rome's most walkable neighbourhood — arrive early and wander Via del Boschetto before sitting down
- The vibe is locals-first, unhurried, slightly cramped in the best way
- Cash preferred, though cards are accepted

cards.title: Tazza d'Oro
cards.address: Via degli Orfani, 84, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
## ☕ Morning Coffee [7:00 – 8:30] | Sant'Eustachio → Pantheon

**Primary:** Sant'Eustachio Caffè
**Alternative:** Tazza d'Oro

**Flow**

- 8–9 min walk from Banco 19 B&B
- Stand at the bar (no table service)
- Order: espresso or cappuccino + cornetto
- Walk straight to Pantheon exterior after

**Why this spot**

- Iconic Roman espresso
- Perfect launch point for Historic Center wandering
- Sets the tone for the day

**Duration:** 30–45 mins
**Cost:** €3–6 per person
**Energy:** Light, intentional

cards.title: Banco 19 B&B
cards.address: Via dei Banchi Nuovi, 19, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes: Check-in starts at 2:00 p.m. on Fri Apr 24. Check-out before 10:30 a.m. on Tue Apr 28.
details.address: Via dei Banchi Nuovi 19, Rome, RM, 00186 Italy

cards.title: Historic Center Wandering — Navona → Pantheon → Trevi → Spanish Steps
cards.address: Piazza Navona, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: 12:00 PM – Piazza Navona: sit by fountains, espresso or light lunch, ease in. 1:15 PM – Pantheon: stand under the oculus, look up in silence, one photo not 50. 2:30 PM – Trevi Fountain: toss coin, 10–15 min max, don't fight for the photo. 3:30 PM – Spanish Steps: climb to top, look back over rooftops, sit 5–10 min. This is your "we're actually here" moment.
details.prep: Comfortable walking shoes, sunglasses, small crossbody bag, water bottle. No reservations needed.
details.tips: Don't rush transitions. Avoid Google Maps too much — follow feel. Skip anything with a huge line. The magic is detours.
details.notes:
🇮🇹 Historic Center Wandering (Navona → Pantheon → Trevi → Spanish Steps)


Main activity: Aimless but intentional afternoon walk through Rome’s most beautiful historic core

Start: 12:00 PM
End: 4:30 PM
Duration: ~4–4.5 hours
Energy: Light, romantic, no tickets, no pressure
Best Day: Friday (wife’s first fully free afternoon)

🎒 Prep (Minimal)

Comfortable walking shoes

Sunglasses

Small crossbody/day bag

Water bottle

No reservations needed

Loose lunch spot bookmarked near Piazza Navona

Goal: Feel like you’re living in Rome, not “seeing” Rome.

👣 Flow (Loose Directional Route)

1️⃣ Piazza Navona (12:00–1:00 PM)
Arrive slowly.

What to do:

Sit by the fountains

Espresso or light lunch

Ease into vacation mode

This is your decompression moment after her conference days.

2️⃣ Pantheon (1:15–2:00 PM)
5-minute walk from Navona.

Inside:

Stand under the oculus

Look up in silence for a minute

Take one photo, not 50

Optional:
Espresso at Sant’Eustachio or gelato nearby.

3️⃣ Trevi Fountain (2:30–3:15 PM)
Crowded but worth it.

Toss coin over right shoulder

Stay 10–15 minutes max

Don’t fight for the perfect photo

This is spectacle energy.

4️⃣ Spanish Steps (3:30–4:30 PM)
End here.

Climb to the top

Look back over rooftops

Sit for 5–10 minutes

This is your “we’re actually here” moment.

🍦 Built-In Micro Stops (Optional)

Gelato anywhere that looks legit

Quick shopping detour on Via del Corso

Duck into random churches (they’re all incredible)

The magic here is detours.

⚡ Optimization Tips

Don’t rush transitions

Avoid Google Maps too much — follow feel

Skip anything with a huge line

Save Trevi + Spanish Steps at night as a bonus revisit if you love them

🍷 Transition to Aperitivo (4:30 PM)

From Spanish Steps area:

Walk toward your aperitivo spot

Or Uber if you’re tired

Reset before dinner
details.energy: Light, romantic, no tickets, no pressure
details.micro_stops: Gelato anywhere that looks legit. Quick shopping on Via del Corso. Duck into random churches.

cards.title: InRome Cooking Classes
cards.address: Corso del Rinascimento, 65, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A hands-on dessert class that functions as both an experience and a warm-up — loose, social, and genuinely fun before a big dinner night in Monti.

**Know before you go**
- Arrive by 5:20 PM — they hold the door for 10 minutes maximum
- Bring your QR code tickets (codes: Rj2Cuba / WT8nDny) — screenshots work fine
- Don't eat a full lunch beforehand; you'll be tasting as you go
- Wear something you don't mind getting flour on — aprons are provided but they're not generous
- Prosecco is served at the start, so the tone is relaxed from the first minute
details.cancellation_policy: Full refund if cancelled 15 working days or more prior. No-shows and late cancellations subject to 100% penalty. All tickets non-refundable.

cards.title: Two Sizes
cards.address: V. del Governo Vecchio, 88, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
One of Rome's best specialty coffee stops — a small, unpretentious café that takes coffee seriously without making it precious.

**Know before you go**
- Order at the bar, not the counter — this is still a Roman café at heart despite the specialty beans
- The flat white and pour-over are both excellent; the cornetti are from a good local supplier
- Via del Governo Vecchio is one of Rome's nicest streets for a slow morning walk — pair it with a wander toward Campo
- It gets busy after 9 AM; go early for a calm start
- Smaller and quieter than Sant'Eustachio — the right choice when you want coffee without the crowd

cards.title: Two Sizes → Termini
cards.address: V. del Governo Vecchio, 88, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: 2 min walk from hotel → Stand at bar → Espresso only (keep it tight) → Head back, grab bags → Taxi to Roma Termini for 9:49 AM train to Frascati.
details.notes:
## ☕ Morning Coffee [7:00 – 8:30] | Quick Espresso Before Termini

**Spot:** Two Sizes
**Base:** Banco 19 B&B

**Flow**

- 2 min walk from hotel
- Stand at bar
- Espresso only (keep it tight)
- Head back, grab bags
- Taxi to Roma Termini for 9:49 AM train

**Why this spot**

- Fastest option
- Zero detours
- Keeps the morning calm before travel

**Duration:** 15–25 mins
**Cost:** €2–4 per person
**Energy:** Efficient, no wandering, logistics-first

cards.title: Frascati Wine Tour — Farmhouse Lunch
cards.address: Via Perazzeta, 30, 00044 Frascati RM, Italy

details.tips: Light breakfast. No heavy dinner Saturday night. Easy Sunday reset after.
details.notes: Suggested to take train at 9:49 AM from Roma Termini station to Frascati station (approx. 20 min ride, tickets cost 2 EUR per person). Activity ends at the same place it began. Mobile tickets available in the GetYourGuide app once payment is processed.
details.address: Piazzale Sandro Pertini, 00044 Frascati RM, Italy
details.includes: Guided Frascati visit, town tasting + snack, transfers to farmhouse, vineyard + cellar tour, 3 wine tastings, farmhouse chef lunch.
details.transport: Train: 9:49 AM from Roma Termini → 20 min ride → ~€2/ticket not included. Arrive 5 min early.
details.meeting_point: Frascati Train Station — Piazzale Sandro Pertini, behind yellow taxi stand.
details.cancellation_policy: Cancel before 10:30 AM on April 25 for a full refund

cards.title: Golden Hour — Aventine → Orange Garden → Keyhole → Trastevere
cards.address: Piazza Pietro D'Illiria, 00153 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
Cinematic Rome moment. Slow, romantic, panoramic sunset over St. Peter's dome.

### 🎯 Intent

Cinematic Rome moment.
Slow, romantic, panoramic sunset over St. Peter’s dome.
Transition from countryside wine to lively Trastevere energy.

---

### 📍 Stop 1: Aventine Hill Arrival

**5:45 PM** – Taxi from hotel (15–20 min buffer)

- Arrive slightly before golden light
- Walk calmly uphill (no rushing)

---

### 🌇 Stop 2: Giardino degli Aranci

**6:00 – 7:15 PM**

- Walk garden perimeter
- Find view framing St. Peter’s dome
- Sit for 10–15 minutes
- Let light shift toward sunset (~7:55 PM in late April)

No photos-first mindset.
Just take it in.

---

### 🔑 Optional Micro-Stop: Knights of Malta Keyhole

Knights of Malta Keyhole

- 5-minute stop
- Quick peek
- Don’t wait if line is long

---

### 🚶 Transition Walk Downhill

**7:15 – 7:45 PM**

Walk downhill toward Trastevere
(Or quick taxi if you prefer conserving energy)

This descent is beautiful in soft light.

---

### 🍷 Aperitivo in Trastevere

**7:45 – 8:30 PM**

- One spritz or glass of wine
- Light snack only
- Keep it casual

cards.title: Da Enzo al 29
cards.address: Via dei Vascellari, 29, 00153 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
One of Trastevere's most honest Roman trattorias — the kind of place that reminds you why you came to Rome in the first place.

**Know before you go**
- Reservation essentially required — if you haven't booked, try calling on the day for a late slot
- Order the cacio e pepe and whatever vegetable contorno is on — the artichokes and chicory are both exceptional
- The room is small and the tables are close; this is not a quiet dinner, it's a lively one
- Trastevere gets busy after 8 PM — book for 7:30 if possible and walk the neighbourhood after
- No theatre here: the food is the entire point

cards.title: Carrefour Express Via Vittoria Store
cards.address: Via Vittoria, 22 A, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Colosseum
cards.address: P.za del Colosseo, 00184 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate unscheduled Colosseum card — the real booking is the 3:30 PM guided tour above. Kept for now so reference data is not lost. Worth deleting post-trip.

**Know before you go**
- See the 15:30 Colosseum card for meeting point, provider, and ticket details

cards.title: Stazione Taxi Navona
cards.address: Piazza delle Cinque Lune, 33, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Nannarella
cards.address: Piazza di S. Calisto, 7/a, 00153 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: ZARA Rome
cards.address: Via del Corso, 189, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Via Soriso, 68a
cards.address: Via Soriso, 68a, 00166 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A saved spot from the discovery phase — a coffee on Via Soriso, paired with the pastry class happening on the same street on Day 4.

**Know before you go**
- Same street as the Saturday pastry class (Via Soriso 68/A) — logical warm-up or cool-down stop on Day 4, not necessarily Day 1
- Small neighbourhood café; not a destination in itself
- Consider moving this card to Day 4 alongside the class, or cutting it if the class already covers the area

cards.title: Hotel NH Collection Roma Palazzo Cinquecento
cards.address: Piazza dei Cinquecento, 90, 00185 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Pizza in Trevi
cards.address: Via della Mercede, 32, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Two Sizes
cards.address: V. del Governo Vecchio, 88, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
Rome's best tiramisu in a deliberate small format — a one-bite ritual, not a full dessert stop.

**Know before you go**
- Two sizes on the menu; small is correct, you do not need the large
- Classic tiramisu is the move, but the pistacchio and fragola versions are genuinely worth trying
- Stand at the counter, no table service — this is a takeaway-style stop
- Cash is appreciated though cards work
- Best paired with a short walk — eat it standing outside, not in motion

cards.title: Pinocchio Toys Rome
cards.address: Via del Tritone, 124, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Antica Farmacia Santa Lucia
cards.address: Via Giulia, 129, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Stazione Taxi Chiesa Nuova
cards.address: Via della Chiesa Nuova, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

cards.title: Two Sizes
cards.address: V. del Governo Vecchio, 88, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate Two Sizes card from trip planning — same place, same notes apply. Worth cleaning up post-trip; leaving both for now so map pins still cluster correctly.

**Know before you go**
- See the other Two Sizes card on this day for the full note

cards.title: Gelateria del Teatro
cards.address: V. dei Coronari, 65/66, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
Small, artisanal, and consistently one of the best gelaterias in Rome — worth a short detour rather than a default stop.

**Know before you go**
- Rotating seasonal flavours change often; pay attention to the board, not the displays
- Sage-raspberry, basil-walnut-honey, and ricotta-fig are signature; ask what is fresh today
- Small size is enough — two flavours, cone, done
- Slightly off the main tourist drag on Via dei Coronari, so calmer than the big-name Giolitti
- Cash is easiest; card works but is slower at busy times

cards.title: Frigidarium
cards.address: V. del Governo Vecchio, 112, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A no-fuss gelateria on Via del Governo Vecchio — the kind of walk-in stop that rewards a slow wander without a plan.

**Know before you go**
- Cone, not cup — and grazie when you order
- Flavours to ask for: pistacchio, nocciola, stracciatella — the classics are where they shine
- Seasonal fruit flavours (fragola in April) are genuinely seasonal here, not from a base
- Queue moves fast; pay at the till first, then order at the counter
- Great at the end of an afternoon walk toward Ponte Sisto or Piazza Navona

cards.title: Flight to Rome — Air Canada
cards.address: Air Canada

details.notes: YYZ → FCO, Non-stop, 8hr35m, Economy Flex. Seat 39C. Aeroplan: 734233398. Ticket: 0142319473573

cards.title: Hotel NH Collection Roma Palazzo Cinquecento
cards.address: Piazza dei Cinquecento, 90, 00185 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A well-positioned base on Via del Corso that puts the entire historic centre within walking distance from the moment you drop your bags.

**Know before you go**
- Via del Corso is a main artery — ask for a room facing the interior courtyard if noise is a concern
- Piazza del Popolo is a 5-minute walk north; the Pantheon is 15 minutes south on foot
- Use the hotel as a midday reset point — it's central enough to pop back between morning and evening without wasting time
- Luggage storage is standard if you arrive before check-in or need to stay late on departure day

cards.title: Colosseum
cards.address: P.za del Colosseo, 00184 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A guided 3-hour walk through the Colosseum, Forum, and Palatine Hill — the ancient-Rome anchor of the trip. Worth the €135 specifically for the Forum and Palatine context, which are hard to read without a guide.

**Know before you go**
- Meet at Oppio Caffè (corner of Via delle Terme di Tito and Via Nicola Salvi) at 3:15 PM — the guide holds a green Walks sign
- Confirmation number: 5ZYRJC9W
- Passport or government ID is mandatory for entry — no exceptions at the gate
- Only small backpacks or handbags permitted; no storage at the site
- Wear proper walking shoes — the Forum and Palatine are uneven ancient stone for 90 minutes
- No refunds for late arrivals or no-shows; cancellation deadline has already passed
- Water bottle and a hat are worth carrying; there is little shade on the Palatine
details.address: 72 Via delle Terme di Tito, Roma, Lazio, 00184, Italy
details.meeting_point: Entrance Colle Oppio Park - (Oppio Caffè), corner of Via delle Terme di Tito and Via Nicola Salvi
details.cancellation_deadline: 168 hours before activity start (free cancellation until then; non-refundable within 5 days of event)

cards.title: Aperitivo — Bar Farnese
cards.address: Vicolo del Gallo, 3, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: 6:30 PM – Aperitivo near Campo de' Fiori or Piazza Navona → Spritz or Negroni + small olives. 8:00 PM – Dinner at a good trattoria (not one of your top two meals yet).
details.notes:
**Intent**
A classic Roman aperitivo to bridge the arrival day — a drink and a slow sit-down before the real first dinner at Roscioli at 8.

**Know before you go**
- You have a short 90-minute window between Colosseum (ends 6:30) and Roscioli (8:00) — this is a drink and olives, not dinner
- Order a Negroni, an Aperol Spritz, or a glass of Frascati; skip the cocktail menu experimentation on night one
- Keep the bill under €25 — you are saving appetite and cash for Roscioli
- The Colosseum → Bar Farnese walk is 25 minutes; grab a taxi if you are jet-lagged
- Not the time for elaborate plates — one small bite maximum
details.order: Cacio e pepe or amatriciana, half bottle wine, tiramisu.

cards.title: Roscioli Salumeria con Cucina
cards.address: Via dei Giubbonari, 21, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
Rome's most iconic deli-restaurant — a tasting-forward meal to land on your first full night, built around the city's best pasta and a natural wine list to match.

**Know before you go**
- Reservation confirmed for 8:00 PM — arrive on time, they seat promptly
- Order: carbonara (non-negotiable), cacio e pepe, and a burrata or salumi board to start
- Ask the sommelier for a natural wine recommendation by the glass or half-bottle; the list is one of the best in Rome
- Counter seating is available for walk-ins on future nights, but you already have a proper table
- Leave room for dessert — their tiramisu and hazelnut semifreddo are worth it
- Pace yourself; this is a 2.5-hour dinner, not a quick bite

cards.title: Night Walk — Pantheon, Navona, Trevi
cards.address: Piazza Navona, 25, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
## Iconic Rome at Night

**Main Activity:** Night Walk — Pantheon, Navona, Trevi
**Start:** 9:30 PM
**End:** 11:00 PM
**Duration:** ~1.5 hours

**Flow**

- Pantheon (lit up)
- Piazza Navona
- Trevi Fountain (after 10 PM = best)
- Optional: Spanish Steps

Walk slow.
No phone scrolling.
Let the city land.

cards.title: Barnum Roma
cards.address: Via del Pellegrino, 87, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A proper specialty coffee spot to open the final full day — slow and deliberate before the Thai massage reset at 10:30.

**Know before you go**
- One of Rome's best specialty roasters; this is the day to order a pour-over or flat white, not just an espresso
- The cornetti and pastries are above average for a coffee bar — fine to eat here rather than detouring for breakfast
- Ten-minute walk from Banco 19; go slow, Monday mornings in Rome are quiet
- If it is busy, Sciascia or Roscioli Caffè are both within 8 minutes as backups
- Keep it to 45 minutes max — Rung Arun starts at 10:30 and is a ~15 minute walk

cards.title: Rung Arun Thai Massage Center
cards.address: Vicolo della Cancelleria, 37, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
# Reflexology Ritual | Rung Arun Thai Massage Center

**Start:** 9:30 AM
**End:** ~11:30 AM
**Duration:** ~2 hours total (including walk + espresso)
**Energy:** Grounded, calm, unhurried
**Goal:** Physical reset → mental clarity → smooth entry into final day

---

## 🦶 Reflexology (9:30–11:00 AM)

**Rung Arun Thai Massage Center**

- Book 90 minutes
- Prefer reflexology + light back/shoulders if available
- Arrive 10 minutes early
- Phones away
- Hydrate before + after

This is the anchor of the morning. No rushing in or out.

---

## ☕ Post-Reflexology Espresso (11:00–11:30 AM)

- Short walk to nearest café
- Stand at bar
- One espresso, no rush
- Transition slowly into afternoon wandering

---

## 🎯 Intent

Monday is the reset before the final dinner. Keep the morning slow, body loose, mind clear. The afternoon wandering and Pierluigi dinner carry the energy — this is the foundation.

cards.title: Afternoon Wandering — Campo → Coronari → Ponte Sisto
cards.address: Via dei Pettinari, 64, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: 1:00 PM – Campo de' Fiori: quick loop, light snack or espresso, 30–45 min max. 2:00 PM – Via dei Coronari: antique shops, beautiful narrow streets, stylish crowd, slow browsing. 3:15 PM – Ponte Sisto: cross the bridge, pause mid-bridge, people-watching, street musicians. 4:00 PM – Drift toward Navona side streets, smaller piazzas, low-key bars, begin transitioning toward aperitivo zone.
details.prep: Comfortable shoes, light bag, no reservations. Plan to end within 10–15 min of Il Pagliaccio.
details.tips: Don't overstay Campo. Keep energy loose. Avoid crossing deep into Trastevere. Stay geographically tight.
details.notes:
## 🌆 Monday Afternoon | Campo → Coronari → Ponte Sisto

**Main activity:** Lively, in-the-mix Rome energy before Michelin night
**Start:** 1:00 PM
**End:** 4:30 PM
**Duration:** ~3–3.5 hours relaxed wandering
**Energy:** Social, stylish, buzzy

---

### 🎒 Prep (Minimal)

- Comfortable shoes
- Light bag
- No reservations
- Plan to end within 10–15 minutes of Pierluigi

Goal: Stay central. Stay fluid. No long transitions.

---

### 👣 Flow

**1️⃣ Campo de' Fiori (1:00–1:45 PM)**
- Lunch or light bite at a nearby spot
- Watch the market wind down
- Energy: open, people-watching

**2️⃣ Via dei Coronari (2:00–3:15 PM)**
- Rome's antique dealer street
- Window-shop, browse galleries
- No pressure to buy

**3️⃣ Ponte Sisto (3:30–4:30 PM)**
- Cross toward Trastevere side
- Sit on bridge wall for a moment
- Watch the river

cards.title: Coronari (Aperitivo)
cards.address: V. dei Coronari, 95/96,97, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A stylish pre-dinner pause on Rome's most atmospheric antique street — the perfect gear-shift between afternoon wandering and a Michelin dinner.

**Know before you go**
- Via dei Coronari is lined with antique dealers and art galleries — arrive 20 minutes early to browse before sitting down
- Order a Negroni or a glass of something local; this isn't a cocktail-list destination, it's a wine-and-aperitivo spot
- Keep it to one drink and light snacks — Pierluigi is a full meal and you want to arrive hungry
- The street is quieter and more elegant than Navona one block over — that's the point
- Early evening light on Coronari is genuinely beautiful; slow down

cards.title: Pierluigi
cards.address: Piazza de' Ricci, 144, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: Leave hotel ~7:40 PM. Done ~10:15 PM. Short night walk after. Easy morning before 12:25 PM flight.
details.notes:
**Intent**
The final night dinner — fresh seafood at a family-run classic near Campo de' Fiori. The last real meal of the trip and the reason to pace yourself on Day 6.

**Know before you go**
- Reservation at 8:00 PM; leave Banco 19 by 7:40 PM, it is an 8-minute walk
- Order strategy: one shared seafood antipasto (the crudo or the vongole veraci), two seafood pastas (the lobster linguine and the spaghetti alle vongole), and optionally a shared grilled fish
- House white by the glass is good; no need to splurge on a bottle if you are done at 10:15
- Budget around €150–200 without wine — this is the priciest meal of the trip, it earns it
- The piazza seating is the move if the weather holds; ask on arrival
- Finish by 10:15 PM, short night walk back to the hotel, flight is 12:25 the next day
details.order: 1 seafood antipasto (share), 2 seafood pastas (vongole/prawn/lobster), optional shared grilled fish, 1 dessert.

cards.title: Caffe Vaticano
cards.address: Viale Vaticano, 100, 00192 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
A 7 AM coffee stop before the Vatican — logistics-first, not destination coffee.

**Know before you go**
- The 1.8 Google rating is real; this is a convenience play, not a quality play. You are here because it is 30 seconds from the meeting point, not because it is good.
- Order a simple espresso at the bar. Do not order anything elaborate.
- If you have 10 minutes of buffer, walk to Sciascia Caffè (5 min away, 4.5 stars, properly good) instead.
- Have your passport and the City Wonders voucher already in hand when you leave — no fumbling at 7:45.

cards.title: Vatican + Sistine Chapel + St. Peter's
cards.address: 00120, Vatican City

details.flow: Arrive by 7:45 AM. Meet guide at bottom of steps across from main Vatican Museums entrance, next to Caffè Vaticano (corner of Viale Vaticano & Via Tunisi). Look for City Wonders blue jacket/polo.
details.tips: 8:00 AM start = ideal crowd advantage. Bring passport/ID. Basilica may close unexpectedly (no refund).
details.notes:
**Intent**
A 3-hour guided tour of the Vatican Museums, Sistine Chapel, and St. Peter's Basilica — the 8 AM start is the single most important logistics call of the trip.

**Know before you go**
- Meet at 7:45 AM at the base of the stairs across from the main Vatican Museums entrance, next to Caffè Vaticano (corner of Viale Vaticano and Via Tunisi) — look for the City Wonders blue jacket or polo
- Confirmation number: 73381618074435
- Passport or valid ID is mandatory at entry; have it in hand, not buried in a bag
- Dress code is strictly enforced: shoulders and knees covered for everyone, no exceptions — wear something that works for St. Peter's not just the Museums
- No large bags or backpacks; small cross-bodies only. Security screening at the entrance
- St. Peter's Basilica can close for religious ceremonies without notice — no refund if it does
- This is a small-group tour (max 20); stay close to the guide, the Museums are a labyrinth
- Non-refundable, non-changeable after booking
details.includes: Skip-the-line Vatican Museums entry, Sistine Chapel, St. Peter's Basilica, expert English-speaking guide, small group max 20.
details.dress_code: Shoulders and knees covered. No large bags. Security screening required.
details.cancellation_policy: Non-refundable and cannot be changed or cancelled after booking.

cards.title: Afternoon Wandering
cards.address: Piazza della Rotonda, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.notes:
**Intent**
Unstructured time in Rome's most beautiful square kilometre — the kind of afternoon that becomes the memory you didn't plan for.

**Know before you go**
- Start at the Pantheon exterior and let direction happen from there — north toward Navona, east toward the Jewish Ghetto, south toward Campo
- Duck into any open church door; Santa Maria sopra Minerva (behind the Pantheon) has a Michelangelo sculpture and almost no queue
- The best gelato in this zone is Della Palma or Giolitti — avoid anything with fluorescent colours piled high
- This area is dense with narrow streets that reward getting slightly lost — put the phone away once you have your bearings
- Save energy for the evening; this is a stroll, not a march

**Intent**
A mid-week dinner at one of Rome's most theatrical pasta spots — the hand-rolled tonnarelli cacio e pepe and the nonne making pasta in the window are the draw.

**Know before you go**
- Reservation confirmed for 7:00 PM (booking code 5025 tc, 1 person) — arrive within 10 minutes or the table is released
- Outside versus inside seating is decided by the waiters on arrival, not by the booking — ask politely when you sit down
- Order: tonnarelli cacio e pepe (the signature), or fettuccine with truffle; skip the starters to save room
- House wine by the carafe is fine here — no need for the full list
- The Cancelleria location is calmer than the Trastevere one; you picked well
- Cash is cleaner at the end of the meal; cards accepted

cards.title: Morning Coffee — Final Roman Espresso
cards.address: Piazza di S. Eustachio, 82, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

details.flow: 8–10 min walk from hotel → Stand at bar → Cappuccino + cornetto → No phones, no rushing → Slow walk back through Campo/Navona.
details.note: Leave for FCO around 10:15–10:30 AM for 12:25 PM flight.
details.notes:
## ☕ Morning Coffee [7:00 – 8:30] | Final Roman Espresso

**Primary:** Sant'Eustachio Caffè
**Alternative:** Caffè Doria
**Base:** Banco 19 B&B

**Flow**

- 8–10 min walk from hotel
- Stand at the bar
- Cappuccino + cornetto
- No phones, no rushing
- Slow walk back through Campo/Navona

**Why this spot**

- Iconic, classic Roman send-off
- Close to hotel
- Keeps morning calm before airport

**Duration:** 30–45 mins
**Cost:** €3–6 per person
**Energy:** Nostalgic, unhurried, present

**Note:** Leave hotel by 8:00 AM latest to allow time before airport transfer.
